chats.text: I appreciate you all holding the line during this period of executive transition. Your resilience is notable.
hey — ryan's 'recalibrate' thing? he was literally on his 3rd tee time at the country club while this was happening. like, golf shoes still on.
the productivity delta we'll see is... significant. we've got to navigate it with precision and purpose.
ryan, your 'downstream applications' of absence is literally just a golf handicap increase. you're fine.
there's a certain elegance in abstracting away the human element of work, isn't there? it allows for cleaner thinking.
historically speaking, this is exactly when quarterly attainment starts to look like a suggestion. ryan's out of office, right? yeah.
the team time prevalence during my absence will require recalibration. i'm looking at the data.
ryan said 'strategically neutralize' — which means he's already told people to work weekends. i saw the calendar invite.
the downstream effect on verticals will be felt across the organization. we must remain agile.
i've been thinking about what a 'deceleration' in executional velocity looks like. it's fascinating.
real question: does anyone have a printed agenda? because the screen's too small to read through 'potential downstream applications'.
ryan just sent me his 'team time prevalence' spreadsheet. it's 47 pages of people working over weekend and on their birthdays. he called it a 'strategic initiative'.
I'm taking a brief respite to recalibrate on downstream applications of my absence. Right?
That deceleration in the productivity delta is historically speaking, inevitable.
The Q3 attainment shortfall for my vertical is... complicated. You know how it works, right?
So what you're saying is we need to reallocate bandwidth across the executive headwinds? Right.
My absence will create a downstream application vacuum. Charles, can you bridge it?
You mean my golf club? The one that's been attaining fairways since last year? You know I can't just—
I appreciate you leaning in here on this strategic neutralization. Right?
His absence is a productivity delta I can't quite... quantify, but I've got the bandwidth to—
You're attaining on what? The team time prevalence metric is... off.
Real question: how do we neutralize the equipment downtime without creating further deceleration?
If you're seeing this, you know what downstream applications means. Right? Be good.
I'm historically speaking... fine with it. But my attainment—
Let's align on the strategic neutralization of our executional headwinds, please.
The downstream applications are... not what they used to be. But we're attaining, right?
Charles, your team time prevalence is creating a delta I can't see. You know the drill.
We'll recalibrate. Right?
{NAME}, my absence creates a delta. I want you to own the downstream applications of that delta.
I'm already on my way out for that 3pm 'quick sync' right?
The team will experience a productivity delta. I need you to neutralize it before the next attestation cycle.
I'll make sure my golf ball trajectory doesn't align with our Q3 attainment shortfall. Right?
{NAME}, I've already allocated bandwidth to verticals that don't require me. You're the downstream application.
{NAME}, I just texted him 'real equipment?' because his cadence is tied to attainment. He didn't get it.
The headwinds are already being neutralized.
{NAME}, he keeps ending with 'right?' like it's a question mark for the whole room. It's exhausting.
{NAME}, your team time prevalence is 17%. I want you to own that delta before the next downstream application.
I've historically speaking neutralized this exact deceleration. Right?
{NAME}, I'm being kind enough to join us. That's the upstream application of my absence.
Does anyone else see that 'equipment?' he just typed? It was in the transcript. Like it was equipment?
{NAME}, the team will experience a productivity delta. I've already allocated bandwidth to verticals that don't require me.
{NAME}, he just ended his last statement with 'right?' I'm not sure if that's a question or an exclamation point. I don't know what to do.
I'm not sure I understand what 'upstream' means in this context. We're downstream.
{NAME}, did you notice Ryan was wearing that same tie as last quarter? I told him we were going for 'fresh leadership energy' but he's like 'the brand is consistency.' I think the board is confused.
You're late. This isn't about you.
I'm actually just getting my golf bag out of the trunk on the way here. Q3 attainment is literally the only reason we're all awake.
So if we're talking about 'team time prevalence' I'd like to see some numbers. Not percentages, just... raw hours spent in the 'right' way.
{NAME}, I swear Ryan's 'recalibrate' just means he's been staring at the same slide deck for 3 hours. Last I heard he's considering a 'strategic pivot' to meditation apps.
So what you're saying is we need a 'bandwidth neutralization' plan for people who can't physically access the bathroom?
You're late to everything. This isn't a joke.
I'm not gonna 'strategically neutralize' anything. I'm just going to enjoy my golf swing. You're welcome.
{NAME}, the board thinks 'upstream' is a new department. They sent me a hiring budget for 'Upstream Operations.' I don't know what they're smoking but it's not fresh leadership energy.
I'm actually just here because the golf course is 40 minutes away and I want to make a good impression on Q3 attainment. You're welcome.
So the board thinks 'upstream' is a department? I told them upstream means before you get to the golf course. They're calling it 'pre-recreational operations.'
I'm not sure if this is a joke but I'm not laughing and my transcript says 'real equipment.' So like... what?
